{"key_event":"Depression crisis, mobilization, and constitutional restoration","reasoning":"Despite Ibáñez’s centralization and Alessandri’s later rebuilding of order and coercive tools, the Depression, 1931-32 breakdown, naval mutiny, and repeated coups left state capacity somewhat below the unusually high 1927 level. Civil society ended stronger than in 1927 because mass parties, unions, students, women voters, and Popular Front organization expanded durable mobilizational capacity, even under repression.","society_change":0.5,"society_power":7.5,"state_change":-0.4,"state_power":8.3}
